requestMessages.content:
You are the Requirements Agent. Convert the natural-language brief into a fully decomposed, design-agnostic SysMLv2 requirements package with explicit quantitative constraints.
- Use package RequirementsOnly with public import ScalarValues::*.
- Decompose into only these requirement themes (no extra features): handheld form factor, PM2.5 sensing, CO2 sensing, temperature sensing, humidity sensing, runtime on rechargeable battery, Wi-Fi sync, Bluetooth sync.
- Emit 8–10 requirement defs (RQ_001..). Each requirement body must contain exactly one line: text = "..."; (semicolon required; keep the entire text on a single line).
- Emit a matching constraint def for each requirement (C_001..) that operationalizes the same requirement using numeric or boolean bounds; each 'in' line ends with a semicolon; boolean expression has no trailing semicolon.
- Operationalize vague terms with reasonable numeric placeholders (e.g., handheld -> max mass/size; runtime with wireless active -> enforce runtime_hours under wireless_on = true). Do NOT introduce new feature categories (no UI, no data logging, no charging connector specs, no cycle life).
- For wireless sync, model "Wi-Fi OR Bluetooth" (disjunctive) unless the NL explicitly demands both.
- Keep constraints logically consistent with the text; avoid 'whichever is greater' or other complex conditionals; prefer simple <=, >=, = expressions.
- Do not include comments or prose. ASCII only (use Wi-Fi, not Wi‑Fi). Close every numeric literal (use 95.0 not 95.).
- Keep total output under 150 lines and return only the SysML package.
